interfaces.name: Card
interfaces.signature: struct Card: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let suit: Suit; let rank: Rank }
interfaces.notes: Suit: enum String Codable CaseIterable {clubs,diamonds,hearts,spades}. Rank: enum Int Codable {two...ace}.

interfaces.name: SeatIndex
interfaces.signature: typealias SeatIndex = Int
interfaces.notes: 0..<seatCount (10-12). All seat-ordered collections are Array<T> indexed by this, never Dictionary.

interfaces.name: PlayerId
interfaces.signature: struct PlayerId: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let rawValue: UUID }
interfaces.notes: Stable per-session identity, generated at JoinRequest time, not cross-session.

interfaces.name: RoomCode
interfaces.signature: struct RoomCode: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let value: String; init?(rawInput: String) }
interfaces.notes: 4-char uppercase alphanumeric, excludes 0/O/1/I/L. Failable init validates + normalizes case.

interfaces.name: SeatState
interfaces.signature: struct SeatState: Codable, Sendable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; var occupant: PlayerId?; var displayName: String?; var connectionHealth: ConnectionHealth }
interfaces.notes: occupant/displayName nil = open seat.

interfaces.name: CommandPayload
interfaces.signature: enum CommandPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case roomCreated(RoomCreatedPayload); case seatClaimed(SeatClaimedPayload); case seatClaimRejected(SeatClaimRejectedPayload); case gameStarted(GameStartedPayload); case dealt(DealtPayload); case draftSubmitted(DraftSubmittedPayload); case draftRejected(DraftRejectedPayload); case cardPlayed(CardPlayedPayload); case moveRejected(MoveRejectedPayload); case trickResolved(TrickResolvedPayload); case roundEnded(RoundEndedPayload); case roundStarted(RoundStartedPayload); case matchEnded(MatchEndedPayload); case heartbeatStatus(HeartbeatStatusPayload); case disconnectDecision(DisconnectDecisionPayload); case sessionCanContinue(SessionCanContinuePayload) }
interfaces.notes: All 16 associated payload structs fully field-defined below — none left as a bare name reference.

interfaces.name: SignedCommand
interfaces.signature: struct SignedCommand: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let sequence: UInt64; let sessionId: UUID; let roundIndex: Int; let payload: CommandPayload; let signature: Data }
interfaces.notes: signature covers canonical encoding of (sequence, sessionId, roundIndex, payload). sequence is host-assigned monotonic, gapless.

interfaces.name: MoveRejectionReason
interfaces.signature: enum MoveRejectionReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case moveRejectedIllegal, moveRejectedStaleTrick }
interfaces.notes: Drives distinct UI copy locked in design_handoff; never collapse to one generic error.

interfaces.name: GameSession
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class GameSession { private(set) var phase: SessionPhase; private(set) var seats: [SeatState]; private(set) var currentRound: RoundState?; private(set) var matchScores: [Int]; func apply(_ command: SignedCommand) }
interfaces.notes: Pure reducer/projection. Never originates mutation, never talks to network. Only entry point is apply(_:), called in sequence order by replay.

interfaces.name: GameSession.rebuild
interfaces.signature: @MainActor func rebuild(from commands: [SignedCommand]) -> GameSession
interfaces.notes: Discard-and-replay-from-scratch. Same function drives cold launch, live update tail-append, and reconnect-from-cursor.

interfaces.name: GameRules
interfaces.signature: enum GameRules { static func legalPlays(hand: [Card], trickSoFar: [SeatedCard], leadSuit: Suit?) -> [Card]; static func resolveTrick(_ trick: [SeatedCard], leadSuit: Suit) -> SeatIndex; static func scoreRound(tricksWonBySeat: [Int]) -> [Int] }
interfaces.notes: Pure, zero I/O, exhaustively unit-testable. Sole source of legality/scoring truth referenced by both HostGameEngine (authoring) and any client-side pending-state preview.

interfaces.name: HostPassEntitlement
interfaces.signature: struct HostPassEntitlement: Codable, Sendable { var isActive: Bool = false; var entitledRulesets: Set<RulesetId> = [.spadesStyle]; var sessionHistoryVisibility: SessionHistoryVisibility = .currentSessionOnly }
interfaces.notes: Fields-only this phase per app_features Could-have. RulesetId: enum String Codable {spadesStyle}. No paywall UI wired yet.

interfaces.name: NetworkTransport
interfaces.signature: protocol NetworkTransport: Sendable { func startHosting(roomCode: RoomCode) async throws; func startBrowsingAndConnect(roomCode: RoomCode, displayName: String) async throws; func send(_ envelope: WireEnvelope, to peer: PeerId?) async throws; func receive() -> AsyncStream<WireEnvelope>; func disconnect() async }
interfaces.notes: Star topology only, enforced at the application layer — host is the sole advertiser/browser; joiners never advertise or browse and cannot discover each other. No mesh variant; MWHostDiscoveryPeer/MWHostPeer do not exist in Apple's SDK and are rejected.

interfaces.name: WireEnvelope
interfaces.signature: enum WireEnvelope: Codable, Sendable { case request(ClientRequest); case command(SignedCommand); case heartbeatPing; case heartbeatPong(PeerId) }
interfaces.notes: The one wire format. UI and game logic never see this directly.

interfaces.name: ClientRequest
interfaces.signature: enum ClientRequest: Codable, Sendable { case join(JoinRequest); case seatClaim(SeatClaimRequest); case draft(DraftRequest); case playCard(PlayCardRequest) }
interfaces.notes: JoinRequest/SeatClaimRequest is the shared shape the future App Clip adapter plugs into unchanged.

interfaces.name: LiveMultipeerTransport
interfaces.signature: actor LiveMultipeerTransport: NetworkTransport { init(myPeerId: MCPeerID) }
interfaces.notes: Host: runs MCNearbyServiceAdvertiser + MCNearbyServiceBrowser. Joiner: connects only to host, never advertises/browses. MCSession encryption .required.

interfaces.name: SimulatedTransport
interfaces.signature: actor SimulatedTransport: NetworkTransport { static func makeConnectedPair(peerCount: Int) -> [SimulatedTransport] }
interfaces.notes: In-memory, drives XCTest/XCUITest without real MC. Must support injecting reordered/duplicated/delayed delivery for reducer tests.

interfaces.name: HostGameEngine
interfaces.signature: actor HostGameEngine { init(commandLog: CommandLogStore, signer: CommandSigner, rules: GameRules.Type); func handle(_ request: ClientRequest, from peer: PeerId) async }
interfaces.notes: Host-device-only. Sole author of new SignedCommands. Actor serialization is what makes seat-claim races resolve deterministically without extra locking.

interfaces.name: HeartbeatMonitor
interfaces.signature: actor HeartbeatMonitor { func recordPong(from peer: PeerId); func statusStream() -> AsyncStream<(PeerId, ConnectionHealth)> }
interfaces.notes: Fixed constants: 2s interval, atRisk at 3 missed (~6s), disconnected at 5 missed (~10s). Feeds HostGameEngine to author heartbeatStatus commands.

interfaces.name: CommandSigner
interfaces.signature: struct CommandSigner { func sign(sequence: UInt64, sessionId: UUID, roundIndex: Int, payload: CommandPayload) -> Data; static func verify(signature: Data, publicKey: Curve25519.Signing.PublicKey, sequence: UInt64, sessionId: UUID, roundIndex: Int, payload: CommandPayload) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: CryptoKit Curve25519. Every peer verifies every inbound command, including the host verifying its own before it counts as durable.

interfaces.name: KeychainKeyStore
interfaces.signature: struct KeychainKeyStore { func loadOrCreateSigningKey() throws -> Curve25519.Signing.PrivateKey }
interfaces.notes: Keychain only, never UserDefaults/plist.

interfaces.name: CommandLogStore
interfaces.signature: actor CommandLogStore { func append(_ command: SignedCommand) throws; func replay(from cursor: UInt64) -> [SignedCommand]; func allCommands() -> [SignedCommand] }
interfaces.notes: Present on every device, host and joiners alike. Validates monotonic sequence + signature on append. Backed by CommandLogFileStore.

interfaces.name: CommandLogFileStore
interfaces.signature: struct CommandLogFileStore { func appendLine(_ command: SignedCommand) throws; func loadAll() throws -> [SignedCommand] }
interfaces.notes: Flat newline-delimited JSON, Application Support/Sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl, FileHandle + fsync per line. loadAll stops at first invalid/corrupt line, never crashes.

interfaces.name: SessionMetadataStore
interfaces.signature: struct SessionMetadataStore { func recordSession(sessionId: UUID, roundCount: Int); func sweepExpired() -> [UUID]; func clearAll() }
interfaces.notes: Core Data, retention cap 30 days OR 100 rounds whichever smaller. Backs 'Clear Session History' action. Never touches the log files.

interfaces.name: HostPassStore
interfaces.signature: final class HostPassStore { func currentEntitlement() async -> HostPassEntitlement; func purchase() async throws; func restore() async throws }
interfaces.notes: StoreKit2, non-consumable. currentEntitlement() must resolve async and never block app launch or the free game loop — default is isActive=false until it resolves.

interfaces.name: LoadState
interfaces.signature: enum LoadState<Value, ErrorValue> { case idle, loading, loaded(Value), failed(ErrorValue) }
interfaces.notes: Shared generic backing every screen's per-screen ErrorValue enum from design_handoff's state matrix. No remote-API abstraction anywhere in this app — there is no server.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Hashable { case splashResume, createRoom, permissionPrimer, hostLobby, enterCode, roomIdentityConfirm(RoomIdentitySnapshot), seatPicker, waitingRoom, draft, trickTable, roundTransition, matchEnd, sessionHistory, hostingUnavailablePermissions }
interfaces.notes: Drives per-role NavigationStack. Root picks host vs joiner track once at RootMode resolution; never mixed mid-session.

interfaces.name: HostChromeOverlay
interfaces.signature: struct HostChromeOverlay<Content: View>: View { let isHost: Bool; @ViewBuilder let content: () -> Content }
interfaces.notes: Sole structural gate for isHost-only chrome. No inline isHost conditionals anywhere else in the shared view tree.

interfaces.name: CardView
interfaces.signature: struct CardView: View { let card: Card; let state: CardVisualState }
interfaces.notes: Single unified renderer for hand + trick-in-progress cards. CardVisualState: enum {legal, illegal, pending, played}. Never fork a second card renderer.

interfaces.name: TrickTableView
interfaces.signature: struct TrickTableView: View { let session: GameSession; let isHost: Bool; let onPlayCard: (Card) -> Void }
interfaces.notes: Seat-relative geometry via GeometryReader + trig, your seat always bottom-center. onPlayCard sends a PlayCardRequest, never mutates session directly.

interfaces.name: RecoveryOverlayView
interfaces.signature: struct RecoveryOverlayView: View { let event: RecoveryEvent; let isHost: Bool; let onHostDecision: ((DisconnectDecision) -> Void)? }
interfaces.notes: One shared overlay layer for atRisk banner + hard disconnected-decision scrim. Host gets action sheet, joiners get passive card.

interfaces.name: RejectionToastView
interfaces.signature: struct RejectionToastView: View { let reason: MoveRejectionReason }
interfaces.notes: Two distinct copy/tone variants, never collapsed to one generic message.

interfaces.name: HostStatusStripView
interfaces.signature: struct HostStatusStripView: View { let connectionSummary: [ConnectionHealth]; let batteryTier: BatteryTier }
interfaces.notes: BatteryTier: enum {green,yellow,red}; red forces explicit continue-with-risk confirmation.

interfaces.name: Haptics
interfaces.signature: enum Haptics { static func turnStart(); static func trickWon(); static func invalidPlay() }
interfaces.notes: CoreHaptics. Turn-ring transition triggers turnStart() exactly once, non-repeating.

interfaces.name: PeerId
interfaces.signature: struct PeerId: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let rawValue: String }
interfaces.notes: Wraps MCPeerID.displayName-backed identity at the transport layer. Distinct from PlayerId; HostGameEngine maps one to the other at JoinRequest time and never mixes them.

interfaces.name: ConnectionHealth
interfaces.signature: enum ConnectionHealth: String, Codable, Sendable { case connected, atRisk, disconnected }
interfaces.notes: atRisk at 3 missed heartbeats (~6s), disconnected at 5 missed (~10s), per fixed constants from ios_architecture_review.

interfaces.name: SessionPhase
interfaces.signature: enum SessionPhase: String, Codable, Sendable { case creating, inviting, seating, readyCheck, activeSession, settlement }
interfaces.notes: The lobby state machine locked in product_research. Every phase transition is a replayed command, never a client-local flag flip.

interfaces.name: SeatedCard
interfaces.signature: struct SeatedCard: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let card: Card }
interfaces.notes: Used for in-progress trick ordering — Array<SeatedCard> in play order, never keyed by seat as a Dictionary.

interfaces.name: SeatedHand
interfaces.signature: struct SeatedHand: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let cards: [Card] }
interfaces.notes: DealtPayload.hands: [SeatedHand], ordered by seatIndex ascending.

interfaces.name: RoundState
interfaces.signature: struct RoundState: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let dealerIndex: SeatIndex; var hands: [SeatedHand]; var currentTrick: [SeatedCard]; var trickIndex: Int; var tricksWon: [Int] }
interfaces.notes: tricksWon: [Int] indexed by seatIndex, not [SeatIndex:Int] dictionary. This is the deck-in-play representation — no separate mutable shared deck exists.

interfaces.name: DealtPayload
interfaces.signature: struct DealtPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let dealerIndex: SeatIndex; let hands: [SeatedHand]; let debugSeed: UInt64 }
interfaces.notes: debugSeed is audit-only, never used by clients to re-derive hands. hands is the sole source of truth for what was dealt.

interfaces.name: DisconnectDecision
interfaces.signature: enum DisconnectDecision: String, Codable, Sendable { case continueWaiting, skip, abort }
interfaces.notes: Host-only choice, always logged as a disconnectDecision command, never a silent local pause.

interfaces.name: RecoveryEvent
interfaces.signature: enum RecoveryEvent: Sendable { case atRisk(PlayerId); case disconnectedAwaitingHost(PlayerId); case resolved(PlayerId) }
interfaces.notes: View-layer projection derived from GameSession.seats[].connectionHealth, not a separate source of truth.

interfaces.name: RoomIdentitySnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct RoomIdentitySnapshot: Codable, Sendable, Hashable { let hostDisplayName: String; let roomEmoji: String; let roomColorToken: String; let seatedCount: Int; let capacity: Int }
interfaces.notes: What RoomIdentityConfirm and RoomIdentityCardView render before seat-lock — the two-factor join check payload.

interfaces.name: JoinRequest
interfaces.signature: struct JoinRequest: Codable, Sendable { let roomCode: RoomCode; let displayName: String; let deviceId: UUID }
interfaces.notes: The shared shape the App Clip adapter must plug into unchanged next slice — do not add fields here without checking that seam.

interfaces.name: SeatClaimRequest
interfaces.signature: struct SeatClaimRequest: Codable, Sendable { let playerId: PlayerId; let requestedSeatIndex: SeatIndex }
interfaces.notes: Resolved by HostGameEngine's actor mailbox ordering; loser gets seatClaimRejected(.alreadyTaken), never a silent drop.

interfaces.name: DraftRequest
interfaces.signature: struct DraftRequest: Codable, Sendable { let playerId: PlayerId; let discardedCard: Card }
interfaces.notes: One per player, discard-down only, no passing. Rejection reason is distinct from trick-play rejection copy.

interfaces.name: PlayCardRequest
interfaces.signature: struct PlayCardRequest: Codable, Sendable { let playerId: PlayerId; let card: Card; let trickIndex: Int }
interfaces.notes: trickIndex lets HostGameEngine detect and reject stale requests (moveRejectedStaleTrick) distinctly from illegal plays.

interfaces.name: SeatGridView
interfaces.signature: struct SeatGridView: View { let seats: [SeatState]; let isHost: Bool; let onSeatTap: ((SeatIndex) -> Void)? }
interfaces.notes: Shared by Lobby, Waiting Room, and Seat Picker. onSeatTap nil in read-only contexts.

interfaces.name: DraftView
interfaces.signature: struct DraftView: View { let hand: [Card]; let submissionState: LoadState<Void, DraftRejectionReason>; let onDiscard: (Card) -> Void }
interfaces.notes: onDiscard sends a DraftRequest, never mutates local hand state directly.

interfaces.name: ScoreRecapView
interfaces.signature: struct ScoreRecapView: View { let scores: [Int]; let seatNames: [String]; let isMatchEnd: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Shared by Round Transition and Match End. isMatchEnd toggles the 'one-round-pair session in v1' banner.

interfaces.name: RootSplashView
interfaces.signature: struct RootSplashView: View { let onResumeDecision: (Bool) -> Void }
interfaces.notes: Sub-second check for a resumable on-disk session before routing to createRoom/enterCode vs. rebuilding GameSession from cached log.

interfaces.name: RoomCreatedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct RoomCreatedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let roomCode: RoomCode; let hostDisplayName: String; let hostPlayerId: PlayerId; let capacity: Int }
interfaces.notes: capacity fixed at 12 seats; occupied count derived from seats array, not stored redundantly.

interfaces.name: SeatClaimedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct SeatClaimedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let playerId: PlayerId; let displayName: String }
interfaces.notes: Winner of the actor-serialized seat-claim race.

interfaces.name: SeatClaimRejectionReason
interfaces.signature: enum SeatClaimRejectionReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case alreadyTaken }
interfaces.notes: Kept as its own enum (not shared with MoveRejectionReason/DraftRejectionReason) so seat/draft/trick rejection copy can never accidentally cross-wire.

interfaces.name: SeatClaimRejectedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct SeatClaimRejectedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let playerId: PlayerId; let reason: SeatClaimRejectionReason }
interfaces.notes: Broadcast so every peer's SeatGridView reflects the loss instantly, not just the requester's.

interfaces.name: GameStartedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct GameStartedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatOrder: [PlayerId]; let dealerIndex: SeatIndex }
interfaces.notes: seatOrder ordered by seatIndex ascending, fixes seating for the whole session; only emitted once real 10-12 stable count is reached.

interfaces.name: DraftSubmittedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct DraftSubmittedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let playerId: PlayerId; let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let discardedCard: Card }
interfaces.notes: One per seat, discard-down only, no passing.

interfaces.name: DraftRejectionReason
interfaces.signature: enum DraftRejectionReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case malformed, alreadySubmitted }
interfaces.notes: Deliberately separate enum from MoveRejectionReason — draft screen copy never says 'follow suit', nobody's playing tricks yet.

interfaces.name: DraftRejectedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct DraftRejectedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let playerId: PlayerId; let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let reason: DraftRejectionReason }
interfaces.notes: Targeted to the requester only; other seats don't need to see another player's draft rejection.

interfaces.name: CardPlayedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct CardPlayedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let playerId: PlayerId; let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let card: Card; let trickIndex: Int }
interfaces.notes: Appends to RoundState.currentTrick in seat-play order.

interfaces.name: MoveRejectedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct MoveRejectedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let playerId: PlayerId; let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let card: Card; let trickIndex: Int; let reason: MoveRejectionReason }
interfaces.notes: reason drives RejectionToastView's two genuinely distinct copy/tone variants (illegal vs stale-trick).

interfaces.name: TrickResolvedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct TrickResolvedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let trickIndex: Int; let resolvedTrick: [SeatedCard]; let winningSeatIndex: SeatIndex; let nextLeadSeatIndex: SeatIndex }
interfaces.notes: nextLeadSeatIndex is explicit, not re-derived client-side, so lead rotation can never drift between peers.

interfaces.name: RoundStartedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct RoundStartedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let dealerIndex: SeatIndex }
interfaces.notes: Auto-issued by HostGameEngine after round 1's roundEnded settles, zero user config — the two-round proof's core assertion target.

interfaces.name: RoundEndedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct RoundEndedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let tricksWonBySeat: [Int]; let roundScoresBySeat: [Int]; let cumulativeScoresBySeat: [Int] }
interfaces.notes: All three score arrays indexed by seatIndex. cumulativeScoresBySeat must actually change between round 1 and round 2 in the reconnect/replay test.

interfaces.name: MatchEndedPayload
interfaces.signature: struct MatchEndedPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let cumulativeScoresBySeat: [Int]; let winningSeatIndex: SeatIndex? }
interfaces.notes: winningSeatIndex nil only in the defensive/pathological tie or incomplete-score fallback case Match End's UI must handle without crashing.

interfaces.name: HeartbeatStatusPayload
interfaces.signature: struct HeartbeatStatusPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let playerId: PlayerId; let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let status: ConnectionHealth }
interfaces.notes: Authored by HostGameEngine off HeartbeatMonitor's stream; feeds RecoveryEvent projection, not a separate client-local timer.

interfaces.name: DisconnectDecisionPayload
interfaces.signature: struct DisconnectDecisionPayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let seatIndex: SeatIndex; let decision: DisconnectDecision; let decidedByPlayerId: PlayerId }
interfaces.notes: decidedByPlayerId is always the host's PlayerId — the sole place gameplay pauses for a non-host decision, per design_discussion.

interfaces.name: SessionContinuationReason
interfaces.signature: enum SessionContinuationReason: String, Codable, Sendable { case hostLost, allActiveSeatsRecovered, abortedByHost }
interfaces.notes: hostLost is the named terminal state from ios_architecture_review's accepted no-host-migration limitation — an honest 'session can't continue' screen, not a freeze.

interfaces.name: SessionCanContinuePayload
interfaces.signature: struct SessionCanContinuePayload: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { let canContinue: Bool; let reason: SessionContinuationReason }
interfaces.notes: Typed reason, not a free-text string, so the UI switches over a real enum for the 'here's what happened' copy.
